The CPA’s Order 81, behind the cover of complicated legal jargon, in effect, turned the food future of Iraq over to global multinational private companies, hardly the liberation most Iraqis had hoped for. texts.full_text: old typewriters Menu Search Skip to content Home About Search for: Oliver No. 5 The 103 Year Old Oliver Twins Some time back, it was Two-Fer Tuesday, and I came into possession of a pair of Oliver No. 5 typewriters. The seller was very glad that the Olivers were going to a loving home. One was in incredibly good condition; it just needed a little cleaning. The other was dirty, rusty, and nonfunctional. These came to me in the winter – a time for sweaters, scarves and Olivers. Finding two Oliver No. 5 typewriters here in the wilds of California is unusual – their geographic territory is much further east of here. It appears that they are both from 1913. One has serial number 285132: The other one has serial number 342169: The Oliver in pretty good shape has a little rust and gunk and types with persuasion. It has a metal cover and wooden base. The other Oliver is non-functional, very rusty, corroded and dirty. It needs a clean up and a new drawband. It should be fine. They’re so cute. They’re holding hands. So I had one for me and one to share. The rusty, nonfunctional Oliver is naturally more interesting to me, so I gave the one in better shape to Good Neighbor Brian. Brian is super supportive when it comes down about finding a new place or getting his typewriters fixed up again—a task which he seems quite enthusiastic and committed towards doing as well! texts.full_text: pain perception and disability level in chronic non-specific low back pain. A randomized, single-blinded (the assessor collecting the outcome data was blinded), controlled trial. A private physiotherapy research clinic. A total of 64 individuals, 20 men and 44 women, mean (SD) age was 51 years (11.93), with low back pain of more than three months, with or without pain radiating to the lower extremities above the knee, were distributed into a control ( n = 20) or an experimental group (n = 44). The experimental group received transregional interferential current therapy in addition to usual care, while the control group only received usual care. Pain intensity and disability were assessed using a visual analog scale (VAS) and the Roland-Morris Disability Questionnaire, respectively, at baseline, immediately after treatment (one week), and one month post-treatment. The results showed significant improvements in pain perception and disability levels for the experimental group compared to the control group. texts.full_text: . The Retinas, will be commemorating the release of their new EP ANDY (Pho Realz Records), with a rip-roaring performance at Ortlieb’s. The band tools within a diverse-range of sonic textures from loose exuberance, to a more personal scaled-back yet powerful reverb-shaking discharge of emotion. Working the sliding scale, gearing up to break free, and gathering those of a similar mind within united-infectious melodies stoked with a psychedelic candle, The Retinas launch into the psych-pop stratosphere that still feels comfortably at home. Joining in the festivities, the quartet of Stoops stitch together tight melodic turns of phrase, gradually heating up as those words stick in one’s head and the body’s tension dissipates. texts.full_text: study of intimate partner violence in women's lives. Eight focus groups of women with recent exposure to intimate partner violence (IPV) were conducted to elicit women's descriptionsIntersections of harm and health: a qualitative study of intimate partner violence in women's lives. Eight focus groups of women with recent exposure to intimate partner violence (IPV) were conducted to elicit women's descriptions of their lived experiences, the ways in which IPV intersected with their health, and the broader social, cultural, and factors shaping their journeys. Participants, recruited from community health centers and shelters, shared narratives that highlighted the complex interplay between physical, emotional, and structural harms and their efforts to navigate health systems, relationships, and societal expectations. Key themes emerging from the data included: 1. The invisibility of harm: Women described how societal norms and stigma often minimized their experiences of violence, leading to feelings of isolation and shame. Many reported that healthcare providers failed to recognize or address IPV, exacerbating their physical and mental health struggles. 2. Health as a double-edged sword: While participants sought medical care for injuries or trauma, they also recounted instances where health systems inadvertently reinforced harm—such as through judgmental attitudes or lack of confidentiality. 3. Resilience and agency: Despite enduring significant trauma, women emphasized strategies of resilience, including support from trusted friends, community resources, and legal advocacy. Their narratives underscored the importance of autonomy and safety in rebuilding health. 4. Structural barriers: Systemic issues, such as poverty, housing instability, and limited access to legal protections, were frequently cited as obstacles to escaping abusive relationships and accessing care. The study underscores the need for trauma-informed, survivor-centered approaches

texts.full_text: awareness as to what NASA is currently doing. On NASA’s website they clearly lay out the goals of the mission, ” testing the limits of human research,performance and physiology in space, demonstrating new technologies and research capabilities, [and] contributing to technology development for future exploration." The combination of traditional media like news coverage and educational resources with newer forms of communication such as social media live streams and interactive Google+ hangouts allowed NASA to reach a wide audience across different age groups and interests. One aspect that stood out to me was the use of social media to give viewers an inside look at the daily lives of astronauts on the ISS, from their meals to exercise routines. This personal touch helped make the astronauts more relatable and fostered a sense of connection with them. Additionally, the educational resources provided through Google+ were incredibly useful for teachers looking to incorporate real-life examples into their lesson plans. I haven't come across any other campaigns that are quite as comprehensive in blending new and traditional media, but I believe "A Year in Space" serves as an excellent model for how organizations can effectively communicate their missions and goals to the public. The success of this campaign demonstrates the power of storytelling and the importance of adapting to different platforms when trying to engage diverse audiences. texts.full_text: his wedding ring around my neck. I also got a tattoo of his pet name for me in his handwriting. He called me Sweetie. I swear, it takes everything I have not to become the painted lady and get his handwriting all over me. Kind of like Angelina Jolie but maybe a bit creepier. Although she wore a vial of blood around her neck, so maybe not. I can feel him rolling his eyes and saying- stop using me as an excuse to get a tattoo. lol I’m not sure why I am writing this out with the intention of posting it. I think it’s partly because I’ve been MIA on my Carpool Confessions page and I wanted to let you know why. I think it’s partly because I feel proud of myself for how I’m handling it and I want to share it. Maybe it can encourage or inspire someone else to look for the blessings. In one of the million books I’ve read, it said something like “In the midst of tragedy can come great beauty.” I’m paraphrasing, but I love that idea. I’ve seen a lot of beauty in the last 4 months. Because of my family, my friends, my community, my kids, my own strength. Connections. I did have to look for it. The sadness can definitely cloud your perception. But I am holding on to my umbrella. And it seems to be working ok. I just wanted you to know. Tell your friends! Facebook Google Email Twitter Like this: Like Loading... Our approach to overcome these challenges is to emulate virus structure through the use of a synthetic gold (Au) nanoparticle (AuNP) conjugated with different functional peptides creating a nanoengineered virus-mimic (NVM). The NVM can carry an antigen of choice in high density. In addition, other functional peptides can also be easily attached to the NVM. For example, peptides that can enable NVMs to enter cells or to activate the immune cells of the body could be attached. It is a further object of the present invention to provide a method and apparatus for depositing material from a gaseous phase onto a substrate surface without requiring a substantial dilution of the photodissociated material. These and other objects of the present invention are achieved by a method and apparatus for depositing material from a gaseous phase onto a substrate surface. The method and apparatus of the present invention comprise a laser source of energy, a substrate surface, and a source of gaseous material. The source of gaseous material is located in proximity of the substrate surface. The source of gaseous material is photodissociated by the laser source of energy to produce a photodissociated material. The photodissociated material is deposited onto the substrate surface. The method and apparatus of the present invention provide a number of advantages. The method and apparatus of the present invention do not require a carrier gas. The method and apparatus of the present invention do not require a vacuum system. The method and apparatus of the present invention do not require a substantial dilution of the photodissociated material. The method and apparatus of the present invention are particularly advantageous for depositing silicon carbide. The method and apparatus of the present invention are also advantageous for depositing silicon dioxide. The method and apparatus of the present invention are also advantageous for depositing silicon. The method and apparatus of the present invention are also advantageous for depositing other materials. texts.full_text: ntuition For Big-data Analysis Big-data analysIs is the process of looking for buried or secret patterns that influence a predictive outcome. Determining which 'patterns' to look for requires much human intuition as you may have seen in the movie "Limitless." Researchers from the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) are planning to take out the need for human intuition in big-data analysis and let computers comb the data for predictive patterns. The MIT researchers planning to take this big risk in big-data analysis are from the Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence Laboratory (CSAIL). The prototype of the software system is called 'Data ScieScience Machine' or DSM, which should not be confused with Data Mining Software as it's completely different. The main goal for MIT researchers here to see if artificial intelligence can take out human intuition from data analysis process and eventually replace them in near future! If successful this will also reduce cost of hiring experts who interpret results after analyzing huge sets manually using t
